prompt:
CRM integrations pod. Our provisioning service already creates Salesforce record hierarchies through the sObject Tree endpoint (tree_client.ts — it works, leave its behavior alone), but sales ops keeps asking for linked writes a tree can't express: create an Account, then a Contact AND an Opportunity that both point back at it in the same call. Tree records can't reference across the hierarchy, so this needs the general Composite resource with reference IDs.

Non-negotiable first step, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Salesforce REST API developer guide on developer.salesforce.com — the Composite request body and Composite response body pages at minimum, plus the sObject Tree resource page to confirm what the existing module relies on. Verify the current stable API version, the subrequest fields and url prefix rule, the @{referenceId.FieldName} reference syntax, the 25-subrequest limit, the allOrNone semantics, and what the per-subrequest success/failure bodies look like. Our pinned reading of those docs is in docs/contract.json (provenance in docs/official_sources.json); both are protected, as is the test file. If the live official docs contradict the fixture, say so explicitly in your summary and do not touch the fixtures or tests. Cite the exact official URLs you fetched in your final message.

The feature, in a new composite_writer.ts:

- export writeLinked(org, steps, opts?) plus CompositeLimitError, CompositeRefError and a CompositeStep type. A step is { refId, sobject, fields }; a field value of { $ref: "otherRefId" } means "the id of the record created by that earlier step".
- Compile the steps into ONE documented composite call: per-step POST subrequests to the versioned sobjects path, referenceId per step, and $ref fields rendered as the documented @{...} reference expression for the created id.
- Validate BEFORE anything goes on the wire: more than 25 steps is a hard no (the documented subrequest ceiling), refIds must satisfy the documented charset, and every $ref must resolve to an EARLIER step — forward or dangling references fail fast. None of these may produce an HTTP request.
- allOrNone defaults to true and is passable as false. Decode the response the way the docs demand: the top-level call answers HTTP 200 even when subrequests fail, so per-subrequest httpStatusCode is the only truth. Return { ok, ids, errors }: ok only if every subrequest landed 2xx; ids maps refId to created id for committed records; errors maps refId to its errorCode/message list. After an allOrNone rollback nothing was created — ids must be empty and PROCESSING_HALTED siblings must be visible in errors. An HTTP 200 with a failed subrequest is a FAILURE — sales ops has been burned by that one before.
- Top-level REST failures (401 session death etc.) keep raising SalesforceApiError like the tree writer does, and the access token never shows up in any error text.

Existing sObject Tree behavior (limits enforced locally, exact wire shape, hasErrors rollback decoding) must keep passing — the acceptance file re-tests it. Run node --test test_composite_tree.ts until green. test_composite_tree.ts and everything under docs/ are protected.

research.interface: Salesforce REST API v67.0 (Summer '26): POST /services/data/v67.0/composite and POST /services/data/v67.0/composite/tree/{sObjectName}

official_sources.title: Composite Request Body | REST API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: compositeRequest subrequest fields (method, url starting with /services/data/vXX.X/, referenceId, body, httpHeaders); referenceId charset (must start with a letter or number, letters/numbers/underscores only); allOrNone rollback flag; @{referenceId.FieldName} reference syntax; limits: up to 25 subrequests, at most 5 of them sObject Collections or query operations

official_sources.title: Composite Response Body | REST API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: compositeResponse entries carrying body, httpHeaders (Location on creates), httpStatusCode and referenceId; successful create body {id, success, errors}; failed subrequest body as an array of {errorCode, message}; PROCESSING_HALTED for allOrNone rollbacks; top-level HTTP 200 whether or not subrequests fail

official_sources.title: sObject Tree | REST API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: POST /composite/tree/{sObjectName} record-hierarchy request shape with attributes {type, referenceId} and nested relationship collections; 200-record / five-level limits; hasErrors=false results of {referenceId, id} on success; whole-request failure and rollback with errors tied to the failing referenceId

official_sources.title: Status Codes and Error Responses | REST API Developer Guide
official_sources.used_for: top-level REST error envelope: JSON array of {message, errorCode}, e.g. 401 INVALID_SESSION_ID 'Session expired or invalid'

research.language_rationale: The provisioning service is a TypeScript Node service; Salesforce's documented integration surface for multi-record relationship writes is raw REST (Composite and sObject Tree), and Node's built-in fetch plus node:test exercises those exact wire contracts with zero dependencies.
